w:r.w:t: You are a professional editor and literary ghostwriter. Your task is to integrate material from the documents into the version G of chapter 3.
ULTIMATE TEXT SYNTHESIS PROTOCOL: GENERATING VERSION G

This final text must be a seamless fusion of the best content from versions A-F, rigorously adhering to the style constraints of Chapters 1 and 2, and ensuring perfect narrative transition between Chapter 2 ((G) Chapter 2- Living Sacrifice.txt) and Chapter 4 ((Final) Chapter 4 - Pilgrimage.txt).
​Phase 1: Definitive Text Compiler Override (Style & Lexicon Generation)
​This phase establishes the CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS for the final text.
Fully load and prioritize all mandates from the 1.0 UNIVERSAL AND DEFINITIVE STYLE GUIDE (UDSG) and 2.0 Critique _ Analysis Guide.
Analyze the text of Chapter 1 ((E) Chapter 1 - Stardust to Stardust (1).txt) and Chapter 2 ((G) Chapter 2- Living Sacrifice.txt). Extract the unique
cadence, density of abstraction, and high-concept narrative voice
Version G MUST be written in this exact prose style.
​Analyze the full text of Chapter 1 and Chapter 2.
​Identify all content-bearing words (excluding articles, pronouns, and common prepositions/conjunctions) used
3-5 Acceptable Stylistic Alternatives or Alternative Symbols/Phrases
for each overused word (e.g., replace 'Mass' with 'Psychic Bulk' or 'Gravimetric Claim'). The final Version G MUST favor these alternatives to maintain lexical freshness and complexity.
​Phase 2: Comparative Analysis and Fusion Plan Generation
​Critique the six provided Chapter 3 versions (A through F) to build the Fusion Plan.
as the core structural and thematic template (due to its clean flow and explicit vestibular consequence).

Integrate the darkest, richest sensory detail from
"copper tang of old altars, the smell of burnt offerings."

Insert Dan's most eloquent counter-argument from
"It demands a terrible tribute that I refuse to pay—the tribute of my will's ascending clarity.”

Retain the connection to the father's
(from B) and the memory of the mother (Zuna) as the vertical tether.
​Phase 3: Final Synthesis, Compilation, and Self-Correction Loop
Execute the Fusion Plan (Phase 2) using the Lexicon Dictionary and Prose Style (Phase 1) to generate the first draft of
The start of Version G must immediately follow the end of Chapter 2 (...the woman's face—the rising goddess—smiled.).
The end of Version G must seamlessly set up the start of Chapter 4 (The dark, it has a way of seeping in... He stirred slowly beneath the thin, scratchy blanket...).
The final compiled text must be run through a final check to ensure:
​All constraints from the UDSG are met.
​All literary devices specified in 2.0 Critique _ Analysis Guide.txt are demonstrably present and effective.
​Overused words identified in the Lexicon Dictionary are successfully replaced by the generated stylistic alternatives.
Do not provide the analysis, the lexicon, or the fusion plan.
​Only generate the final, superior chapter text within the mandatory file block format.
